finding: The peer runtime durability workflow referenced a nonexistent filesystem-terminal-journal.mjs path and omitted the actual durable journal and filesystem store modules, so a future run would fail before exercising the intended durability contract.
design_implication: CI source bindings must be treated as an executable dependency graph and regression-tested against repository paths, not trusted as static workflow text.
changes: .github/workflows/frontier-peer-runtime.yml now watches and syntax-checks durable-peer-terminal-journal.mjs and filesystem-peer-terminal-store.mjs

tools/frontier-research/frontier-peer-runtime-workflow.test.mjs verifies required paths exist, stale paths are absent, both tests execute, permissions remain read-only, and action references remain immutable-SHA pinned
uncertainty: The connector did not return a GitHub Actions run for the test commit.
The new regression test was not executed in a local or hosted Node runtime during this run.

Run the Frontier peer runtime durability GitHub Actions workflow; a missing-file or test failure falsifies clean-runner readiness.
